goal: Fix scanner export classification: (a) nested functions inside exported parents inherit exported:true (buildToolHandlers.inboundByKind false-stales the honest coderef-mcp-server.ts header via exports_match_ast); (b) export-const declarations with multi-line initializers (e.g. export const X = new Set([...])) are not extracted as exported elements, so @exports headers cannot list real const exports. Rolling program: P2 fixes the vitest .mjs collection failure (STUB-Z1ETZD), P3 purges ghost index entries (STUB-81XNNM).

tasks.description: Localize the exported-flag propagation and const-declaration extraction in the scanner/extractor; reproduce both: buildToolHandlers.inboundByKind exported:true in index.json AND BUILTIN_RECEIVERS/JS_GLOBAL_CALLEES/JS_PROTOTYPE_METHODS absent from call-resolver AST exports

Failing tests scanner-export-classification.test.ts: nested function inside an exported function -> exported:false; export const X = new Set([...]) (multi-line) -> constant element present with exported:true; plain non-exported const stays unexported
Fix exported-flag propagation (nested elements get their own flag, never the parent's) + extract exported multi-line const declarations as elements
npm run build && repopulate; verify header_stale_count 1 -> 0 (mcp-server false-stale clears), header_coverage rises, element-count delta recorded with paired evidence
npx vitest run - full suite green
Restore BUILTIN_RECEIVERS/JS_GLOBAL_CALLEES/JS_PROTOTYPE_METHODS to call-resolver @exports header (now honest); MCP validation_status clean; SCHEMA.md baseline + CHANGELOG refresh
Commit Phase 1 + push to main; phase transition complete
